+1. [Enable role-based access control on Azure AI Search](search-security-enable-roles.md).
+

records.raw_commit_message:
docs(search): add missing RBAC enable step in knowledge base Configure access section (#766)

* docs(search): add note to enable RBAC before using Microsoft Entra ID with knowledge bases

The Configure access > Use roles section shows DefaultAzureCredential
usage but does not warn that Azure AI Search is created in apiKeyOnly
authentication mode by default. Without enabling role-based access
first, any Microsoft Entra ID credential is rejected with a 403
Forbidden error regardless of role assignments.

Add an IMPORTANT callout before the SDK-specific steps that links to
search-security-enable-roles.md and explains the default behaviour.

* docs(search): convert RBAC callout to numbered step per reviewer feedback

Replace [!IMPORTANT] alert with an explicit numbered step inside each
zone pivot block (C#, Python, REST). Step 1 now links to
search-security-enable-roles.md, making the enable-roles requirement
actionable rather than advisory.

Addresses review feedback from haileytap on PR #766.
records.summary: 1. Enable role-based access control on Azure AI Search.
records.title: docs(search): add missing RBAC enable step in knowledge base Configure access section

records.change_summary: When you create this knowledge source with contentExtractionMode set to standard, you might get the following error. To resolve the error, define the default values as instructed in the Content Understanding prerequisites. Afterwards, you can proceed with creating the knowledge source.
records.patch_excerpt:
{"total_files_in_commit":4,"files":[{"filename":"articles/search/agentic-knowledge-source-how-to-blob.md","status":"modified","additions":5,"deletions":2,"patch":"@@ -582,10 +582,13 @@ To surface document-embedded images (such as diagrams or scans) in answer synthe
 
 ## Known errors
 
-If you get the following similar temporary error when creating a knowledge source with `contentExtractionMode` with `Standard` as a value:
+When you create this knowledge source with `contentExtractionMode` set to `standard`, you might get the following error.
+
+```json
 Failed to create custom analyzer 'azs_tmp': BadRequest - {"error":{"code":"InvalidRequest","message":"Invalid request.","innererror":{"code":"DefaultsNotSet","message":"Defaults have not yet been set. Call 'PATCH /contentunderstanding/defaults' first."}}}
+```
 
-Refer to [this Content Understanding setup](/azure/ai-services/content-understanding/tutorial/create-custom-analyzer?tabs=portal%2Cdocument&pivots=programming-language-rest#prerequisites) so you can define the default values, so you can proceed with the knowledge store creation, right after.
+To resolve the error, define the default values as instructed in the [Content Understanding prerequisites](/azure/ai-services/content-understanding/tutorial/create-custom-analyzer?tabs=portal%2Cdocument&pivots=programming-language-rest#prerequisites). Afterwards, you can proceed with creating the knowledge source.
 
 ## Related content

 After the knowledge source exists, up"},{"filename":"articles/search/agentic-knowledge-source-how-to-onelake.md","status":"modified","additions":5,"deletions":2,"patch":""},{"filename":"articles/search/agentic-knowledge-source-how-to-sharepoint-indexed.md","status":"modified","additions":5,"deletions":3,"patch":""}]}

records.change_summary: The listed fileName is taken from the Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="..." header on the upload request. REST calls and the.NET SDK set this header directly, while the Python SDK accepts a filename parameter.
records.patch_excerpt:
{"total_files_in_commit":1,"files":[{"filename":"articles/search/agentic-knowledge-source-how-to-file.md","status":"modified","additions":1,"deletions":3,"patch":"@@ -308,7 +308,7 @@ The following ingestion parameter properties control how uploaded files are proc
 
 After the knowledge source exists, upload files directly to it. Each upload is a synchronous call: Azure AI Search extracts content from the uploaded file, chunks the content, creates embeddings when needed, and prepares the extracted content for retrieval before the call returns. You don't have to configure or run a separate ingestion pipeline.
 
-The request body contains the file content. The listed `fileName` is taken from the `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="..."` header on the upload request. If the header isn't set, the service assigns an auto-generated `fileName`. SDKs can set the header through the upload method parameters shown in the following examples.
+The listed `fileName` is taken from the `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="..."` header on the upload request. REST calls and the .NET SDK set this header directly, while the Python SDK accepts a `filename` parameter and builds the header automatically. If the header isn't set, the service assigns an auto-generated `fileName`.
 
 ::: zone pivot="csharp"
 
@@ -445,8 +445,6 @@ A response includes metadata for each uploaded file. The `errorMessage` value is
 }
 ```
 
-**Reference:** [Knowledge Sources - List Files](/rest/api/searchservice/knowledge-sources/list-files?view=rest-searchservice-2026-05-01-preview&preserve-view=true)
-
 Because uploads are synchronous, a file is ready for retrieval as soon as its upload call succeeds. If processing fails, the upload response and any subsequent list entry include a non-`null` `errorMessage`. Review the value for unsupported file types, extraction failures, model access issues, or quota limits.
 
 ## Delete uploaded files"}]}
records.summary: The listed fileName is taken from the Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="..." header on the upload request. REST calls and the.NET SDK set this header directly, while the Python SDK accepts a filename parameter.

 ::: zone-end

{"total_files_in_commit":1,"files":[{"filename":"articles/search/agentic-knowledge-source-how-to-file.md","status":"modified","additions":11,"deletions":2,"patch":"@@ -369,7 +369,12 @@ Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="installation-guide.pdf"

 ```
 
-**Reference:** [Knowledge Sources - Upload File](/rest/api/searchservice/knowledge-sources/upload-file?view=rest-searchservice-2026-05-01-preview&preserve-view=true), even if you reuse the same `fileName`. Each upload creates a new file with its own `fileId`, so the list of uploaded files can contain multiple entries that share a `fileName`.
+**Reference:** [Knowledge Sources - Upload File](/rest/api/searchservice/knowledge-sources/upload-file?view=rest-searchservice-2026-05-01-preview&preserve-view=true)
+
+::: zone-end
+
+> [!NOTE]
+> Uploading a file doesn't replace an existing file, even if you reuse the same `fileName`. Each upload creates a new file with its own `fileId`, so the list of uploaded files can contain multiple entries that share a `fileName`.
 >
 > To replace content, delete the prior file by `fileId` before or after the new upload.

-A response includes metadata for each uploaded file.The `errorMessage` value is `null` when the upload is processed without an error.

@@ -487,6 +492,10 @@ api-key: {{api-key}}

+::: zone-end
+
+## Assign to a knowledge base
+
 If you're satisfied with the knowledge source, [add it to a knowledge base](agentic-retrieval-how-to-create-knowledge-base.md).
 
 ## Query a knowledge base"}]}
